name: GLSL TOP
description: The GLSL TOP renders a GLSL shader into a TOP image. Use the Info DAT to check for compile errors in your shaders.
summary: The GLSL TOP renders a GLSL shader into a TOP image. Use the Info DAT to check for compile errors in your shaders.

parameters.name: GLSL Version
parameters.label: GLSL Version
parameters.description: Pick what version of GLSL to compile the shader with.
glsl120 - glsl330 - glsl400 - glsl410 - glsl420 - glsl430 - glsl440 - glsl450 - glsl460 -

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description:
mode -  - Choose what type of shader you are writing, vertex/pixel shader, or a compute shader.

 vertexpixel - compute -

parameters.name: Vertex/Pixel Shader
parameters.label: Vertex/Pixel Shader
parameters.description: vertexpixel - compute -

parameters.name: Preprocess Directives
parameters.label: Preprocess Directives
parameters.description: predat - Use this  to place preprocessor directives at the start of your shader, such as #extension. This is required since these need to be the first lines in the shader, and TouchDesigner will be adding code to the start of your shader to declare uniforms/functions which would appear before #extension directives located in the main shader. #include statements should be avoided unless include-guards are in the DATs. This should not be used for pre-prending common code to your shaders. Use #include statements in the main shader to do that.

parameters.name: Vertex Shader
parameters.label: Vertex Shader
parameters.description: vertexdat - Points to the DAT holding the  . Drag & Drop a  here, or manually enter the path to the .

parameters.name: Pixel Shader
parameters.label: Pixel Shader
parameters.description: pixeldat - Points to the DAT holding the Pixel . Drag & Drop a  here, or manually enter the path to the .

parameters.name: Compute Shader
parameters.label: Compute Shader
parameters.description: computedat - Points to the DAT holding the Compute Shader. Drag & Drop a  here, or manually enter the path to the .

parameters.name: Load Uniform Names
parameters.label: Load Uniform Names
parameters.description: loaduniformnames - When this button is pressed the node will try to pre-fill all it's uniform parameter with uniforms that are declare in the shader. Note that the shader compiler will likely not expose uniforms that are unused.

parameters.name: Auto Dispatch Size
parameters.label: Auto Dispatch Size
parameters.description: autodispatchsize - Automatically set the dispatch size based on the compute shader's local size and the output texture resolution. Ensures at least one thread per pixel will execute.

parameters.name: Dispatch Size
parameters.label: Dispatch Size
parameters.description:
dispatchsize -  - The dispatch size to use when executing a compute shader.

 dispatchsizex - dispatchsizey - dispatchsizez -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: dispatchsizex - dispatchsizey - dispatchsizez -

parameters.name: Output Access
parameters.label: Output Access
parameters.description:
outputaccess -  - Controls how the output textures will be accessed. If the textures will be read from (such as using previous frame's values), then the access should be changed to Read-Write instead of Write Only.

 writeonly - readonly - readwrite -

parameters.name: Write Only
parameters.label: Write Only
parameters.description: writeonly - readonly - readwrite -

parameters.name: Output Type
parameters.label: Output Type
parameters.description:
type -  - Specify what type of texture to create. When creating  a 3D texture the  will render once for every slice of the output. Refer to  3D Textures and 2D Texture Arrays for more info.

 texture2d - Creates a 2D texture. texture2darray - Creates a 2D Texture Array. Slices of the array can be access using a non-normalized integer index for the w coordinate. texture3d - Creates a 3D Texture. Slices of the array can be accessed using the w coordinate in the range 0-1. Value of the texture in between slices are interpolated.

parameters.name: 2D Texture
parameters.label: 2D Texture
parameters.description: texture2d - Creates a 2D texture. texture2darray - Creates a 2D Texture Array. Slices of the array can be access using a non-normalized integer index for the w coordinate. texture3d - Creates a 3D Texture. Slices of the array can be accessed using the w coordinate in the range 0-1. Value of the texture in between slices are interpolated.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description:
depth -  - Set the depth of the 3D texture from the Input or the Custom Depth parameter.

 input - custom -

parameters.name: Input
parameters.label: Input
parameters.description: input - custom -

parameters.name: Custom Depth
parameters.label: Custom Depth
parameters.description: customdepth - Manually set the depth of the 3D texture, otherwise it will use the depth of the input.

parameters.name: Clear Outputs
parameters.label: Clear Outputs
parameters.description: clearoutputs - When using a Compute , this controls if the output texture should be cleared at the start of each cook. Otherwise the data from the previous cook will stay in the texture, and pixels can be left as-is in the Compute .

parameters.name: Clear Value
parameters.label: Clear Value
parameters.description:
clearvalue -  - The color value to clear all pixels to at the start of the cook.

 clearvaluer - clearvalueg - clearvalueb - clearvaluea -

parameters.name: Input Mapping
parameters.label: Input Mapping
parameters.description:
inputmapping -  - Determines how the node's input(s) are passed into the shader for use when creating a 3D Texture. By default all of the inputs are passed to each slice. When using the N inputs per Slice mode, the first N inputs are passed to the first slice, the next N inputs are passed the second slice, and so on. When it runs out of inputs it loops back to the first input. N is selected by the parameter N Value.

 all - ninputs -

parameters.name: All Inputs to Every Slice
parameters.label: All Inputs to Every Slice
parameters.description: all - ninputs -

parameters.name: N Value
parameters.label: N Value
parameters.description: nval - Determines how many inputs are passed to the shader per slice when using the N inputs per Slice mode for Input Mapping. If for example this is set to 2, then the first 2 inputs will be passed to the first slice, the next 2 inputs will be passed the second slice, and so on. It will loop back to the start of the inputs if it runs out before it reaches the last slice.

parameters.name: Input Extend Mode UV
parameters.label: Input Extend Mode UV
parameters.description:
inputextenduv -  - Controls what is returned from your texture sampling functions when the U and V texture coordinates (called S and T in the shader) are outside [0-1] range.

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

parameters.name: Input Extend Mode W
parameters.label: Input Extend Mode W
parameters.description:
inputextendw -  - Controls what is returned from your texture sampling functions when the W texture coordinate (called W in the shader) are outside [0-1] range. Only useful for 3D Texture.

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

parameters.name: # of Color Buffers
parameters.label: # of Color Buffers
parameters.description: numcolorbufs - Any shader you write can output to more than one RGBA buffer at a time. Turn up this value to have more color buffers allocated for you, and refer to [Write_a_GLSL_#Outputting_to_Multiple_Color_Buffers Write a GLSL ] for more information on using this feature.

parameters.name: Vector
parameters.label: Vector
parameters.description:
vec - Sequence of vector uniforms



  vec0name - The uniform name, as declared in the shader



  vec0value -  - The value(s) to give the uniform.

 vec0valuex - vec0valuey - vec0valuez - vec0valuew -

parameters.name: Name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: vec0name - The uniform name, as declared in the shader

parameters.name: Value
parameters.label: Value
parameters.description:
vec0value -  - The value(s) to give the uniform.

 vec0valuex - vec0valuey - vec0valuez - vec0valuew -

parameters.name: Array
parameters.label: Array
parameters.description: Sequence of array uniforms

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description:
array0type -  - The data type of the uniform in the shader.

 float - vec2 - vec3 - vec4 -

parameters.name: float
parameters.label: float
parameters.description: float - vec2 - vec3 - vec4 -

parameters.name: CHOP
parameters.label: CHOP
parameters.description: array0chop - The channels from this  will be sent to the GLSL shader.

parameters.name: Array Type
parameters.label: Array Type
parameters.description:
array0arraytype -  - The type of the uniform.

 uniformarray - All GPUs can send array data into a GLSL shader using Uniform Arrays. texturebuffer - Newer GPUs can send array data into a GLSL shader using Texture Buffers. Texture Buffers use texture memory and texture fetches to access the data, which allows them to store many more values.Declare them:



And sample them like this



Where i is the 0-based index (an integer) into the buffer that you want to get a value for.

parameters.name: Uniform Array
parameters.label: Uniform Array
parameters.description:
uniformarray - All GPUs can send array data into a GLSL shader using Uniform Arrays. texturebuffer - Newer GPUs can send array data into a GLSL shader using Texture Buffers. Texture Buffers use texture memory and texture fetches to access the data, which allows them to store many more values.Declare them:



And sample them like this



Where i is the 0-based index (an integer) into the buffer that you want to get a value for.

parameters.name: Matrix
parameters.label: Matrix
parameters.description:
matrix - Sequence of matrix uniforms



  matrix0name - The name of the matrix uniform.



  matrix0value - The value to assign the matrix. For valid ways to specify this, see the Matrix Parameters article.

parameters.name: Atomic Counter
parameters.label: Atomic Counter
parameters.description:
ac - Sequence of atomic counter uniforms



  ac0name - The name of the uniform.



  ac0initvalue -  - Specifies how the atomic counters receive their initial value, either through a single default value or a .

 val - chop -

  ac0singlevalue - Specifies a single value that all atomic counters in this binding will be initialized to.



  ac0chopvalue - A reference to the  that will determine the initial values of the atomic counters in this binding. The  will be spanned in track order, so the values from the first track will be read in order first, then the next track (if there is one) and so on. If there are more initial values to fill than there are values in the  then they will all be set to 0. Atomic counters will be initialized from low to high offsets.

parameters.name: Initial Value Type
parameters.label: Initial Value Type
parameters.description:
ac0initvalue -  - Specifies how the atomic counters receive their initial value, either through a single default value or a .

 val - chop -

parameters.name: Single Value
parameters.label: Single Value
parameters.description: val - chop -

parameters.name: Initial Value
parameters.label: Initial Value
parameters.description: ac0singlevalue - Specifies a single value that all atomic counters in this binding will be initialized to.

parameters.name: Initial Values CHOP
parameters.label: Initial Values CHOP
parameters.description: ac0chopvalue - A reference to the  that will determine the initial values of the atomic counters in this binding. The  will be spanned in track order, so the values from the first track will be read in order first, then the next track (if there is one) and so on. If there are more initial values to fill than there are values in the  then they will all be set to 0. Atomic counters will be initialized from low to high offsets.

parameters.name: Constant
parameters.label: Constant
parameters.description:
const - Sequence of constant uniforms



  const0name - The constant name, as declared in the shader.



  const0value - The value to give the constant.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. Making this larger than 1 is essentially the same as taking the output from each pass, and passing it into the first input of the node and repeating the process. Other inputs and parameters remain the same for each pass.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
